title: HEP Proxy Validation: Establishing the Heartbeat-Evoked Potential as a Πⁱ Index
description: This empirical prerequisite protocol establishes the heartbeat-evoked potential (HEP, 250–400 ms window) as a valid trial-by-trial proxy for interoceptive precision Πⁱ before any downstream EP-1 through EP-6 analyses are interpreted. Three sub-predictions are tested: that HEP amplitude correlates with an orthogonal Πⁱ index (heartbeat discrimination d′) independent of any EEG measure (Pred 0.A); that pharmacological elevation of acetylcholine via physostigmine increases HEP amplitude in a dose-dependent manner confirmed by pupillometric engagement (Pred 0.B); and that anterior insula (aINS) BOLD signal tracks HEP amplitude trial-by-trial within participants after controlling for arousal (Pred 0.C). All three predictions must pass before HEP is used as a Πⁱ proxy in downstream protocols. Failure of any Pred 0 criterion is a submission-blocking issue for all protocols referencing HEP.

sub_predictions.claim: HEP amplitude correlates with orthogonal Πⁱ index (heartbeat discrimination d′) — independent of any EEG measure
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: r(HEP, interoceptive d′) > 0.35, p < 0.01, two-tailed; replicated r ≥ 0.25 in independent validation subsample (N ≥ 30)
HEP amplitude increases ≥15% vs. placebo (Cohen's d ≥ 0.50, BF₁₀ ≥ 100); pupil constriction confirms target engagement
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: No HEP change despite verified ACh elevation (pupil constriction confirmed)

falsifying_thresholds.condition: No HEP change when pupil constriction confirms physostigmine target engagement

sub_predictions.claim: Anterior insula (aINS) BOLD tracks HEP amplitude trial-by-trial
sub_predictions.confirming_evidence: r(HEP amplitude, aINS BOLD) > 0.30 within participants after arousal covariate control
sub_predictions.falsifying_evidence: aINS BOLD and HEP statistically independent after arousal covariate

validation_notes: Empirical prerequisite for all downstream protocols referencing HEP as a Πⁱ proxy. Failure of any sub-prediction (0.A, 0.B, or 0.C) constitutes a submission-blocking issue requiring protocol amendment before EP-1 through EP-6 results are submitted.
submission_blocking_status: PREREQUISITE — all three predictions must pass before HEP is used as Πⁱ proxy in EP-1 through EP-6

inclusion_criteria: Resting heart rate 50–100 BPM with regular sinus rhythm
No history of cardiac arrhythmia or autonomic neuropathy
Willingness to complete two sessions (main + replication subsample)
exclusion_criteria: Irregular ECG rhythm (assessed by study nurse at screening)
History of neurological or psychiatric disorder (DSM-5 criteria)
Use of beta-blockers or other HR-modifying medication
Contraindication to physostigmine: glaucoma, bradycardia, active asthma, peptic ulcer disease, urinary obstruction
Inability to maintain fixation for 30-minute EEG blocks

physostigmine_arm_criteria.design: Double-blind, placebo-controlled, crossover (physostigmine 1.0 mg i.v. vs. saline placebo)
physostigmine_arm_criteria.monitoring: Continuous ECG, blood pressure, pulse oximetry during infusion; physician present

apparatus.EEG: 64-channel BrainAmp DC (Brain Products GmbH) at 1000 Hz, referenced to FCz, re-referenced offline to average mastoids
apparatus.ECG: BrainAmp ExG bipolar lead-II at 1000 Hz, synchronised to EEG clock
apparatus.pupillometry: Eyelink 1000 Plus (monocular, 500 Hz) for pupil diameter as arousal covariate and physostigmine engagement check
apparatus.fMRI_for_0C: 3T Siemens Prisma, 64-channel head coil; multi-band EPI TR = 1000 ms, TE = 30 ms, 2 mm isotropic (separate sub-session for Pred 0.C)
apparatus.cardiac_phase_algorithm: R-peak detection via Pan-Tompkins (real-time), HEP epoch −200 to +800 ms post R-peak
apparatus.heartbeat_discrimination_task: Modified Schandry task: participants count heartbeats during silent intervals (15, 25, 35, 45, 50 s); d′ computed from hit rate and false alarm rate against reference ECG

target.type: Heartbeat discrimination trial: silent interval with ECG-verified heartbeat counting (Pred 0.A); resting EEG + physostigmine infusion (Pred 0.B); resting fMRI + EEG simultaneously (Pred 0.C)

trial_structure.response: Pred 0.A: verbal count of perceived heartbeats per interval. Pred 0.B: no task (resting state, passive). Pred 0.C: passive resting state fMRI.

blocks.pred_0a_trials: 6 heartbeat discrimination intervals × 2 sessions (main + replication subsample)
blocks.pred_0b_sessions: 2 (physostigmine vs. placebo, counterbalanced, separated by 7-day washout)

eeg_analysis_pipeline.ica_components: Automated rejection of cardiac and ocular ICA components (EEGLAB ADJUST)

fmri_analysis_pipeline_pred_0c.motion_correction: ICA-AROMA denoising; scrubbing at FD > 0.5 mm
fmri_analysis_pipeline_pred_0c.GLM_regressor: Single-trial HEP amplitude (250–400 ms, Cz) as parametric modulator of BOLD signal
fmri_analysis_pipeline_pred_0c.roi: Anterior insula (aINS): anatomical mask from AAL2 atlas
fmri_analysis_pipeline_pred_0c.nuisance_regressors: 6 motion parameters, CSF signal, WM signal, cardiac noise (RETROICOR)
fmri_analysis_pipeline_pred_0c.arousal_covariate: Mean pupil diameter per TR, RMSSD per 30-s window

dependent_variables: HEP mean amplitude (250–400 ms) at Cz, FCz, Fz (all predictions)
r(HEP amplitude, heartbeat discrimination d′) — primary sample
r(HEP amplitude, heartbeat discrimination d′) — validation subsample (N ≥ 30)
HEP amplitude change: physostigmine vs. placebo, % and Cohen's d (Pred 0.B)
Pupil constriction onset time and magnitude: physostigmine vs. placebo (engagement check)
r(HEP amplitude, aINS BOLD) within participants after arousal covariate control (Pred 0.C)
primary_hypothesis: HEP amplitude in the 250–400 ms window serves as a valid proxy for interoceptive precision Πⁱ, evidenced by (1) correlation with orthogonal heartbeat discrimination d′ > 0.35 replicated across two independent samples, (2) significant increase under physostigmine-induced ACh elevation confirmed by pupillometry, and (3) within-participant tracking of anterior insula BOLD signal after arousal covariate control.
primary_analysis: Pred 0.A: Pearson r between HEP amplitude and heartbeat d′; one-tailed test (directional) with pre-specified threshold r > 0.35; replicated in subsample. Pred 0.B: paired t-test (physostigmine vs. placebo) on HEP amplitude; Bayes Factor via JZS prior (r = 0.707). Pred 0.C: first-level GLM with single-trial HEP as parametric modulator; second-level t-test on aINS beta estimates; partial correlation controlling for pupil diameter and RMSSD.

power_analysis.based_on: Canales-Johnson et al. (2020) HEP–interoception correlation r ≈ 0.38; Filippetti et al. (2022) physostigmine HEP effects d ≈ 0.55

apgi_parameters.hep_pi_i_mapping: HEP₂₅₀₋₄₀₀ = a₀ + a₁·Πⁱ + ε_HEP; a₁ > 0 required for proxy validity

ethics.committee: Institutional Review Board (includes pharmacology sub-board for physostigmine arm)
ethics.physostigmine_ind: Physostigmine administered under IND or equivalent local regulatory approval; physician present during infusion

caveats: Pred 0.B requires a separate pharmacological sub-session; if physostigmine arm cannot be completed (regulatory or safety reasons), Pred 0.B is downgraded to 'deferred' and does not block submission provided Pred 0.A and 0.C both pass.
Pred 0.C requires simultaneous EEG-fMRI, which introduces MRI gradient artifacts in EEG; ICA-based artifact removal is mandatory before HEP computation in scanner.
Heartbeat discrimination d′ is subject to demand characteristics; task instructions must avoid mentioning heartbeats to prevent voluntary respiratory synchronisation.
